block_00: Take a look at the top news of the week
block_03: Engineering simulation software provider ANSYS has reported Q4 2022 earnings that surpassed market expectations, with an EPS of $2.18 and revenue of $669.2 million. ANSYS CEO Ajei Gopal credited the company's strong performance to the global adoption of digital transformation. ANSYS stock closed up 4.5% at $413.29 on Tuesday, with expectations of further gains.
block_07: AMC Entertainment Holdings Inc. received a $304.8 million investment from Mudrick Capital, causing a 10% surge in share price. The cinema chain plans to acquire, refurbish theaters, and deleverage its balance sheet, leading to a 12% rise this week.
block_10: The company is facing a "murky" legal battle with the US SEC over its lending product, according to analysts. The crypto exchange's shares fell 3% on Tuesday as investors remained cautious about regulatory scrutiny, with total losses of 13% in one week.
